item.title: Broken valuation metrics?
item.description: Maybe stocks are not that expensive after all

item.title: Psychedelic drugs are going mainstream
item.description: Commercial success is looking less theoretical for treatments based on mushrooms and LSD

item.title: The wealthy Londoners betting on a luxury retirement
item.description: Riverstone is pioneering club-like housing schemes for asset-rich over-65s

item.title: Pointers for Burnham on what Britain really wants
item.description: The new prime minister can learn from 40 years of polling the UK population

item.title: Why Canada isn’t listening to American whining in the wine wars
item.description: Donald Trump’s tariffs have taught us an important lesson in the value of retaliation

item.title: Rich people hate giving up control, even if it means paying more tax
item.description: ‘Should they hand over assets now or leave everything to be sorted out on death?’

item.title: Big Tech’s AI backstops risk ignominy
item.description: Big benefactors offer their balance sheets as an emergency ‘deep pocket’, enabling unproven AI companies to borrow cheaply

item.title: Andy Burnham’s tenure begins with troubling signals
item.description: Britain needs to revive animal spirits, not return to a statist past

item.title: Labour has handed Burnham a blank cheque
item.description: The new premier’s MPs think they have backed a winner but have no idea what his programme will be

item.title: Don’t blame colonialism for Africa’s trade woes
item.description: The continent’s growth is being hemmed in by blocked trade arteries, not arbitrary borders drawn in Berlin in 1885

item.title: The Middle East needs its own Helsinki Act
item.description: Trust is not a prerequisite for peacemaking — and Iran and its neighbours should follow the example of cold war détente

item.title: How I got into Berghain, Berlin’s notoriously selective club
item.description: The mystique of its intimidating door policy still convinces people to fly in from all around the world

item.title: Trump is slipping into an Iran quagmire
item.description: America cannot achieve its aims by negotiation. So regime change is back on the agenda

item.title: Bad cabinet appointments will steal Burnham’s precious hours
item.description: Incoming leader would do well to take Rishi Sunak’s advice on maximising your time as prime minister

item.title: How the UK can be cleverer about stamp duty
item.description: Complaints about stamp duty are as old as the tax

item.title: Portfolio construction in the shadow of the AI bubble
item.description: Hard choices on all sides

item.title: Training AI models might be the chance for a workplace power play
item.description: It is becoming clear that the knowledge they require is currently locked inside employees’ heads

item.title: Burnham must remember the first rule of government
item.description: The biggest mistake of all would be to imagine the country enjoys a free fiscal lunch. In fact, it only has hard choices

item.title: UK must focus on insiders vs outsiders not north vs south
item.description: Economists and politicians want to boost growth, but it must include people on the periphery of hotspots

item.title: It’s time for Europe to embrace air conditioning
item.description: More frequent hot summers will harm productivity and claim lives

item.title: India’s rupee problem has been misdiagnosed
item.description: Currency weakness reflects subdued private investment and uncertainty over the business climate

item.title: Burnham should pursue a devolution ‘big bang’
item.description: If done right, radical decentralisation could boost UK productivity growth and living standards

item.title: Why ‘rightwing’ is no longer such a dirty word in Britain
item.description: When words like fascist and Nazi are thrown around, being merely conservative seems mild by comparison

item.title: AI should have senior lawyers sharpening their hunting spears
item.description: More adoption of artificial intelligence will lead to more calls to cut fees

item.title: The art of knowing when to step down
item.description: Only a few master one of the hardest career tricks of them all
